title: ricinus communis f. scaber
intro: Ricinus communis f. scaber is a variant of the Ricinus communis plant, also known as the wild castor oil plant. This plant is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain ricin and ricinoleic acid. The ricinoleic acid in Ricinus communis f. scaber has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. Ricinus communis f. scaber can be prepared as a herbal remedy through cold pressing of its seeds to extract castor oil, which is often used in creams, salves, and ointments for topical application.
